references.summary: A long-form personal portfolio that uses a compact anchor navigation, oversized identity typography, an activity-image ribbon, principle-led capability sections, a restrained project navigator, and a direct contact close.
references.asset_reuse_notes: Pattern only. No brand assets, copy, illustrations, source code, or full layout are reused.
strengths_to_adopt: A short anchor navigation makes a long page understandable before scrolling.
One oversized identity statement creates a clear first-screen position.
Life and activity imagery makes the person visible beyond a project list.
Capability sections explain operating principles before presenting project links.
A contact section and social links provide a decisive end to the narrative.
weaknesses_to_avoid: A measured 19,462-pixel page and repeated image loops create substantial scroll cost.
Motion and decorative repetition can overshadow evidence and accessibility.
A full visual or information-architecture clone would erase the target person's identity.
references.target_area: Home narrative, shared shell, activity gallery, capability explanation, archive navigator, and contact close

patterns.name: Measured asymmetric opening stage
patterns.description: A 1320px content axis and approximately 123px fixed header-plus-ticker frame a viewport-height hero whose desktop composition uses a 320px narrative rail, a 64px gap, and a flexible right-aligned identity field.
patterns.applicability: Recreate the spatial relationship with original Home markup and CSS, then collapse at 1180, 900, 640, and 430px without horizontal overflow.

patterns.name: Large but bounded identity type
patterns.description: The primary statement uses a 62-88px responsive range at approximately 0.92 line height while supporting copy stays small and quiet.
patterns.applicability: Use explicit bilingual phrase lines and a narrower 32-58px mobile range so Korean and English retain semantic breaks.

patterns.name: Narrative anchor rail
patterns.description: A small set of in-page destinations previews the story and supports quick jumps.
patterns.applicability: Home sections for identity, proof, journey, mentoring, research, archives, and contact.

patterns.name: Identity-scale hero
patterns.description: A single large role statement is paired with compact editorial metadata and one supporting paragraph.
patterns.applicability: A bilingual AI-native platform lead statement with current role and public links.

patterns.name: Human evidence ribbon
patterns.description: A horizontal sequence of approximately 189-by-237-pixel frames shows learning, community, systems, and making as one life narrative.
patterns.applicability: Use ten original software-system SVG placeholders in a duplicated seamless track, then replace them one-for-one with the user's public activity photos later.

patterns.name: Headline ticker under the primary navigation
patterns.description: A narrow moving line beneath the main navigation introduces the site's vocabulary before the hero, while the header remains fixed.
patterns.applicability: Use original AI-native, device, gateway, API, and verification phrases; pause the track on hover and disable motion when reduced motion is requested.

patterns.name: Sticky media stage with scroll-linked drift
patterns.description: A taller scroll stage holds the media ribbon in view while its track moves horizontally, creating depth without adding another content section.
patterns.applicability: Use one requestAnimationFrame driver to map vertical stage progress to lateral track travel, keep a slow CSS fallback before JavaScript initializes, and provide a static non-sticky layout for reduced motion.

patterns.name: Sticky depth convergence
patterns.description: A tall opening stage keeps one viewport frame pinned while its primary statement moves from distant scale and lower contrast into a crisp reading position, then lifts gently toward the next chapter.
patterns.applicability: Map clamped vertical progress to deterministic scale, translation, and opacity values on the original Home statement; keep the effect desktop-only, disable it for reduced motion, and preserve the semantic document order.

patterns.name: Three-beat aperture convergence
patterns.description: Playwright sampling measured the capability title at scale 3.8 with opacity 0 before entry, scale 2.1626 with opacity 1 during convergence, and scale 1 at the reading position. A black viewport-sized sticky frame clips the oversized edges while the text settles inward.
patterns.applicability: Use a milder original range for haakusi: stage-wide content begins slightly oversized and dim, settles at scale 1, then releases smaller and softer toward the next section. Pair it with subtle side shades rather than copying the reference composition.

patterns.name: Outgoing-incoming scene handoff
patterns.description: The initial scene translates key elements upward by about 90px while fading them to zero, while the next scene enters from roughly 32px below and resolves to full opacity. Only one narrative state is dominant at a time.
patterns.applicability: At selected Home boundaries, fade and lift the outgoing heading while the next section resolves from depth. Keep body copy readable and never animate navigation or links out of reach.

patterns.name: Black-stage clipping frame
patterns.description: The reference keeps a full-viewport black sticky surface with overflow hidden so oversized layers disappear beyond the scene instead of producing layout overflow.
patterns.applicability: Add an original pointer-events-none side vignette and overflow clip to desktop Home scenes, with zero opacity on mobile, light theme, print, and reduced motion.

patterns.name: Compact shared shell rhythm
patterns.description: A compact identity rail and secondary navigation establish one stable content axis before page-specific heroes begin.
patterns.applicability: Use the same 70-pixel identity rail, 54-pixel navigation rail, 1320-pixel content axis, and readable type floor across Portfolio, CV, Research, Blog, Lectures, and Reading.

patterns.name: Circular capability geometry
patterns.description: Large circular and wedge forms turn a capability list into memorable visual anchors without relying on copied brand illustrations.
patterns.applicability: Pair six original conic-gradient dials with AI, JVM, C++, TSX, MCP, and AWS marks, then move the oversized track laterally inside a sticky 220vh desktop stage.

patterns.name: Principles before projects
patterns.description: The site explains how the person thinks before asking visitors to inspect work samples.
patterns.applicability: Position device-to-cloud context, AI-native verification, and product ownership before selected evidence.

patterns.name: Monochrome editorial rhythm
patterns.description: Black, white, and muted grey rely on typography, borders, and spacing rather than decorative gradients.
patterns.applicability: Unify Home, CV, Portfolio, Research, Blog, Reading, and Lectures with one sans-serif token system.

references.summary: A Korean recruitment resume flow that leads with a compact career thesis and presents the remaining evidence as continuous semantic sections.
references.asset_reuse_notes: Pattern only. No personal data, brand assets, source code, private resume URL, or visual layout is reused.
strengths_to_adopt: A short career thesis precedes detailed history and gives the reader a lens for later evidence.
Sections follow a familiar resume order and remain part of one continuous document.
Plain dividers and restrained typography keep dense content readable without a card for every item.
weaknesses_to_avoid: Editing controls, completion widgets, and private profile fields do not belong in a public CV.
Long undifferentiated summary paragraphs can repeat evidence already present in experience.
references.target_area: Public CV profile summary, semantic section order, and compact supporting evidence

patterns.name: Career thesis before detail
patterns.description: One concise statement explains the candidate's trajectory and current value before the reader enters the chronology.
patterns.applicability: Use a device-boundary-to-AI-platform profile paragraph followed by a small fact ledger.

patterns.name: Continuous resume document
patterns.description: Semantic sections are separated by rules and headings rather than repeated floating containers.
patterns.applicability: Use one content axis for experience, education, research, leadership, recognition, and skills.

references.summary: A traditional Korean resume presentation that uses reverse chronology, a stable date rail, and strong company/role labels for fast scanning.
references.asset_reuse_notes: Pattern only. No personal data, brand assets, source code, private resume URL, or visual layout is reused.
strengths_to_adopt: A stable left date rail makes career sequence and tenure immediately visible.
Company and role labels lead each row while details remain subordinate.
Horizontal dividers provide scan anchors without introducing decorative surfaces.
weaknesses_to_avoid: A large skill-chip cloud consumes space without proving depth or outcomes.
Private demographics, salary preferences, and administrative fields should not appear in a public developer CV.
references.target_area: Professional experience chronology, compact rows, and technical foundation

patterns.name: Date rail plus evidence body
patterns.description: Every career row keeps time in a fixed-width rail and reserves the wider column for scope, actions, and outcomes.
patterns.applicability: Use the same rail for employers, education, research, mentoring, and recognition.

patterns.name: Role-first reverse chronology
patterns.description: The newest and most relevant role appears first, with older roles compressed but still visible as the foundation of the trajectory.
patterns.applicability: Keep Suprema expanded and HBrain/AtData concise, preserving the device-to-platform growth path.

references.notes: Private career-document screenshots reviewed locally; no source asset or wording is copied into the public site.

patterns.observation: A viewer should understand role identity and the strongest differentiator within the first screen.
patterns.adaptation: Use one concise identity statement, a restrained evidence summary, and direct paths to portfolio and CV.
patterns.avoid: Competing oversized headings, decorative slogans, or several equally loud calls to action.

patterns.observation: Consistent card widths, a stable left edge, and generous whitespace make dense career evidence easier to scan.
patterns.adaptation: Use one shared content width, an 8-point spacing rhythm, and equal-height grids only where the content is comparable.
patterns.avoid: Independent page widths, floating controls that cover content, and ornamental two-tone panels.

patterns.observation: Short headings and controlled line lengths preserve meaning better than very large display type.
patterns.adaptation: Use a Korean-capable system sans stack, balanced English headings, keep-all Korean headings, and explicit phrase-level hero lines.
patterns.avoid: Forced breaks inside Korean words, aggressive English hyphenation, and serif display type that changes character by page.

patterns.observation: Strong career documents expose the judgment process, not only the final screen or technology list.
patterns.adaptation: Keep problem, decision, implementation, evidence, and current status visibly separated in representative work.
patterns.avoid: Tool inventories presented as impact or unqualified claims without status and evidence.

patterns.observation: A narrow neutral palette leaves emphasis for evidence and state rather than decoration.
patterns.adaptation: Use graphite, paper white, muted text, and one steel accent with borders instead of gradients and heavy shadows.
patterns.avoid: Warm and cool accent colors competing within the same page or different palettes for each navigation tab.

references.notes: GitHub's official guidance for presenting a developer profile alongside a resume.

patterns.observation: A concise bio, relevant skills and experience, and a small set of strongest projects are easier to evaluate than an undifferentiated activity archive.
patterns.adaptation: Keep career-first navigation and route the first screen to three representative work systems, the public CV, and GitHub evidence.
patterns.avoid: Treating every repository or artifact as equally important.

references.notes: Current hiring-manager-oriented resume guidance used only to validate scan order and trajectory framing.

patterns.observation: Experience becomes more persuasive when the reader can see increasing scope, ownership, and relevance to the target role.
patterns.adaptation: Use reverse chronology and explicitly connect device integration, platform ownership, AI-native delivery, and technical leadership.
patterns.avoid: A long skill inventory with no relationship to decisions, outcomes, or target roles.

references.notes: Google Search Central requirements for ProfilePage structured data and discoverability.

patterns.observation: A public profile page should identify one main person, use valid Person data, and be discoverable through normal crawl paths and a sitemap.
patterns.adaptation: Add conservative ProfilePage/Person JSON-LD to Home, canonical URLs to roots, and public sitemap and robots files.
patterns.avoid: Structured-data claims that are not visible or verifiable on the page.

references.notes: WCAG 2.2 used as the accessibility baseline for the shared career interface.

patterns.observation: Reflow, contrast, meaningful sequence, visible focus, and adequate interaction targets are baseline product quality rather than optional polish.
patterns.adaptation: Keep semantic source order, visible skip links and focus rings, 44-pixel controls, readable line lengths, and horizontal-overflow checks at mobile widths.
patterns.avoid: Color-only states, clipped navigation, hidden focus, and layout-dependent reading order.
